rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel And For Sanctions; And Amended Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY JUNE 21 2012, LINE 1, PLAINTIFF ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG, JUNA GOLDBERG'S (Amended) Motion To Compel And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. It would be helpful to the Court for the parties to be prepared to discuss, for each of the Questions in the Separate Statement of Items in Dispute, whether the holding of County of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (Martinez) (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 1446 precludes a response to the question. Pro Tem Ernst Halperin discloses to the parties that he is a Co-Chair of the Advisory Council to the 360 Center at USCF Parnassus. The 360 Center is an HIV primary care clinic. The Advisory Council serves as a fundraising group for the clinic. The parties are encouraged to raise any concerns they may have regarding this disclosure. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Deposition Questions For Defense Witnesses Liz Caserza (4/13/12); Juanchito Crisostomo (4/3/12); Stephen Cummings (4/27/12); Scott Johnson (4/13/12) And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY JUNE 21 2012, LINE 2, PLAINTIFF SALMA KHAN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Deposition Questions For Defense Witnesses Liz Caserza (4/13/12); Juanchito Crisostomo (4/3/12); Stephen Cummings (4/27/12); Scott Johnson (4/13/12) And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. An employee with specialized knowledge about his defendant employer's policies and procedures can be questioned, without being designated as an expert, regarding those policies and procedures and their application to facts with which he or she is acquainted. But if the witness is not designated as an expert, the witness cannot be questioned with regard to the application of policies and procedures to facts with which he or she is not acquainted, as opinions in that regard are strictly within the realm of expert opinion testimony - and thus such questions are not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Plaintiff's motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Deposition of Scott Johnson - denied in its entirety. Deposition of Liz Casera - granted. Deposition of Juanchito Crisostomo - denied with respect to Certified Questions 4 and 5, granted with respect to Certified Questions Nos. 1, 2, 3, 6, and 7, but the questions and answers to questions 3, 6, and 7 shall be designated as "Protected Material" pursuant to the March 21, 2011 Stipulated qualified Protective Order in place in this litigation. Deposition of Stephen Cummings - denied with respect to Question 1, granted with respect to questions 2 and 3. Both parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FERMIN SOLIS ANIEL et al VS. T.D. SERVICE COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses And Monetray Sanctions Againsterlinda Abibas Aniel
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY JUNE 21 2012, LINE 3, DEFENDANT AMERICAN HOME MORTGAGE SERVICING, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses And Monetary Sanctions Against Erlinda Abibas Aniel. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Within 14 days of entry of this order, all Plaintiff' shall provide verified supplemental responses to Request Nos. 1, 7, 12, 15, 17, 19 and 20. In the event Plaintiff has no further responsive documents in Plaintiff's possession, custody or control, the verified responses must state so. Plaintiff shall also produce any additional documents in Plaintiff's possession, custody, or control, including but not limited to bank statements from September 2008 through January 2009; copies of all rental agreements concerning the Property. Sanctions are awarded against all plaintiffs jointly and severally in the amount of $1,462.50. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

KONTRABECKI, TRUSTEE OF THE JTK TRUST, TKG CALIFORNIA I, LLC, THE JTK TRUST AND JOHN KONTRABECKI'S Notice Of Motion For A New Trial. Denied. The Notice of Intention to Move for a New Trial was untimely under Code of Civil Procedure section 659. Moreover, Defendants fail to show that they were prejudicially surprised or that the court erred in granting summary judgment in favor of the plaintiff. rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For A Protective Order Staying Pltf'S Taking Of Witness Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 21, 2012, Line 9. DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S Notice Of Motion For A Protective Order Staying Plaintiff'S Taking Of Witness Depositions. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Thornley as follows: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Granted with respect to a stay of all discovery served by either party for a period of 20 days from date of the order, during which time the parties are ordered to meet and confer concerning a stipulated protective order regarding confidentiality of patient information. Denied with respect to a stay for purposes of setting "a deposition schedule that will not prejudice Defendants." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton For An Order Compelling Defts To Produce The Person Most Knowledgeable For The Deposiiton Scheduled For 4-30-12 And Produce All Requested Docuemtns
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 21, 2012, Line 9. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Defendants To Produce The Person Most Knowledgeable For The Deposition Scheduled For 4-30-12 And Produce All Requested Documents. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Thornley as follows: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Granted with respect to compelling the 18 depositions and PMK deposition(s) within a 120 day period commencing on the day following expiration of the stay. Granted with respect to rescheduling plaintiff's deposition to occur within the same 120 day period. The parties may extend the 120 day period by written stipulation. Denied with respect to waiver of all objections for defendant's failure to serve responses to request for production associated with the PMK deposition. The responses to requests for production to be served per CCP based on rescheduled PMK deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
